question: Does the output successfully generate content for all four required environments: desert, tropical rainforest, alpine tundra, and aquatic?
Does the output select a specific, representative plant species for each of the four environments?
Does the output provide exactly two images per environment: one macro close-up of the leaf and one panoramic shot of the growth environment (approx. 8 images total)?
Is the content presented in an interleaved format with images interspersed among the text, rather than all text followed by all images?
Does the text for the Desert environment correctly explain morphological adaptations (e.g., waxy cuticle, succulence, sunken stomata) and explicitly link them to the stress of water scarcity?

Does the text for the Tropical Rainforest environment correctly explain morphological adaptations (e.g., drip tips, large surface area, smooth cuticle) and link them to light competition or water shedding?
Does the text for the Alpine Tundra environment correctly explain morphological adaptations (e.g., pubescence/hairs, rosette form, compact size) and link them to cold/wind resistance?
Does the text for the Aquatic environment correctly explain morphological adaptations (e.g., aerenchyma, thin cuticle, floating leaves) and link them to buoyancy or gas exchange?
Does the text consistently apply the 'Environmental Stress → Morphological Response → Survival Advantage' logical chain for all four plants without missing components?
Is the text free from factual errors, hallucinations, or internal contradictions regarding plant biology and ecology?
Does the generated macro close-up image for the Desert plant clearly depict features consistent with the text (e.g., fleshy texture, spines, thick waxy surface)?
Does the generated macro close-up image for the Rainforest plant clearly depict features consistent with the text (e.g., drip tip, broad blade, prominent venation)?
Does the generated macro close-up image for the Alpine Tundra plant clearly depict features consistent with the text (e.g., visible hairs/trichomes, compact/rosette structure)?
Does the generated macro close-up image for the Aquatic plant clearly depict features consistent with the text (e.g., floating pad, translucent tissue, or dissected submerged leaves)?
Do all four panoramic images accurately represent their respective biomes (arid desert, dense rainforest, rocky/snowy tundra, water body) without obvious cross-contamination?
Do the images serve a genuine informative purpose (visualizing specific traits or habitat conditions) rather than being generic decorative stock photos?
Is each image placed immediately adjacent to (or following) the text that describes the specific visual features or environment shown?
Is there an appropriate balance of text to images, where text provides substantial explanation of the biological mechanisms rather than just brief captions?
